# Grid rows hold both cell-id generations after column drag

## Context

Found during the live Neural Link debugging session on the operator's devindex (2026-06-11 ~22:56–23:30Z, Chrome session `948c9bba`), supporting the #12883 family. Lane split recorded by the parent's owner (@neo-fable, A2A `da99e2e2`): the **cell-id doubling defect is this leaf, mine**; the overdrag echo-runaway and the view-level region-body DOM tiling are hers (#12929 family). The operator visually confirms the symptom (most plainly: the locked-end column rendering twice).

`Release classification:` operator's call — the grid-corruption family was flagged release-blocking in the 2026-06-11 triage override (#12696 thread); this leaf surfaced post-cut-prep. Defaulting boardless pending the operator's read.

## The Problem

After grid column-header drag resorting, **every checked row in all three region bodies carries cells under BOTH cell-id schemes simultaneously** — the pooled ordinal scheme (`<rowId>__cell-N`) and the field-named scheme (`<rowId>__<dataField>`):

| Region | Columns | Cells found | Composition |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `body-2` row-0 (locked start) | 3 | 6 | `__cell-0/1/2` + `__id`/`__rank`/`__login` |
| `body-1` row-0 (center) | 33 | 34 | `__cell-0..23` (pool window) + 10 field-named |
| `body-3` row-0 (locked end) | 1 | 2 | `__cell-0` + `__lastUpdated` |

Crucially `vdom == DOM` on every check (NL `verify_component_consistency`): the doubling lives **in the App Worker vdom itself** — the SSOT is corrupted, so every subsequent diff reconciles against a poisoned baseline. User-visible: duplicated/overlapping cell content, most obvious in the 1-column locked-end region.

## The Architectural Reality

Both id schemes are deliberate, coexisting in source at HEAD:
- **Pool scheme**: `src/grid/Row.mjs:444` + `:476` create `${me.id}__cell-${poolIndex}`; `src/grid/Body.mjs:944` maps `columnIndex % me.cellPoolSize`; **`Row.mjs:424` REWRITES `oldNode.id` in place to the pool id** — a live migration step and the prime seam candidate.
- **Field scheme**: `src/grid/Row.mjs:225` fallback — `if (!cellId) cellId = me.getCellId(column.dataField)` → `Body.mjs:922`.
- `Body.mjs:1019-1053` (`getDataField`) is the dual-scheme inverse — the engine expects both schemes to exist, but **per cell, not per row**.
- Trigger context: the post-drag redistribution re-render (`onColumnLockChange` → re-sort/redistribute → body re-renders). The corrupting drag's trace also shows `dup`-marked double-logged move events (possible double-dispatch) — relevant because the `:424` in-place rewrite racing or running twice is exactly the shape that strands a generation.

**Hypothesis (to bisect, not assumed):** the post-drag re-render emits cells under one scheme while the other generation's nodes are still mounted; the vdom differ matches by id, no id matches across schemes, so neither set evicts the other — both persist.

**Evidence-baseline caveat:** the live evidence was captured against the operator's dev-server build (post-`#12908`, pre-`#12929`-branch `aafa9cc8b` checkpoint fixes). The bisect must establish behavior on current dev FIRST, then diff against the session build state.

**Documented secondary (same family, fold-or-split at fix time):** cross-body row-window desync — all three bodies report `availableRows: 16` / `startIndex: 0` yet mount 18/22/22 rows; each internally consistent, the invariant broken between bodies.

## The Fix

Bisect-first, fix-second:
1. Instrument/bisect when the `Row.mjs:225` field-id fallback fires vs the `:424` pool rewrite during (a) initial render, (b) post-drag redistribution, (c) horizontal scroll re-render.
2. Identify which post-drag path renders the second generation over the first's living nodes.
3. Fix shape (post-bisect): one canonical id scheme per row render pass + a reconciliation guard that evicts the foreign-scheme generation; regression unit test per the custom unit config.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause documented with a deterministic repro (which path emits the second generation, and when).
- [ ] After column drag + redistribution, every row holds exactly one cell per visible column under one id scheme — verified in all three regions.
- [ ] Regression unit test green via the custom unit Playwright config (NOT bare `npx playwright test`).
- [ ] Secondary row-window desync either resolved by the same fix (with evidence) or split into its own leaf with the captured data.

## Out of Scope

- The overdrag echo-runaway + monotonic scroll-term guard and the `dup` double-dispatch hunt — @neo-fable's lane (#12929 family; her #12908 follow-up).
- The view-level region-body DOM tiling (vdom believes 3 bodies, DOM holds up to 17) — hers, evidence on #12929.
- SortZone landing-index correctness (`end from:1 to:27` mis-targeting) — downstream of her echo fix.

## Related

- Parent: #12883 (native sub-link set post-create). Sibling: #12929. Echo lineage: `#12906` / `#12908`.
- Evidence A2A chain: package `fd07a007`, addendum `c6ac06f4`, lane split `da99e2e2` (Memory Core, team-readable).
- NL anchors: session `948c9bba`, drag trace zone `neo-grid-header-toolbar-sortzone-3` startedAt `1781218587829` (ring buffer preserved), consistency outputs for `body-1/2/3__row-0`.

Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 20 open issues at 2026-06-11T23:33Z; no equivalent found (#12929 = scrollLeft clamp desync, different layer; #12883 = locked-end re-home, the parent). A2A in-flight sweep: clean — the lane was explicitly handed to me by the parent owner.

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-11T23:48:30Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ **Operator repro evidence (@tobiu, 2026-06-12, live devindex testing) — recorded by the lane owner; this is the bisect's repro recipe.**
> 
> Setup: devindex with the **lock config removed from all columns** (no locked regions — important: the defect family does NOT require locks).
> 
> **Repro A — direction reversal within one drag:** drag the narrow `total` column over the much wider `company` column → `company` slides left correctly (first pass animates). Still within the same drag, move back left → **the `company` HEADER moves back right, but its body CELLS do not move back.** Header/cell motion desyncs on reversal.
> 
> **Repro B — overdrag scroll area:** after a reload, drag right into the overdrag-scroll region → **no body cells move with animation at all** (operator doubts they move, period) while header switching proceeds.
> 
> **Control:** on `main` (single body, no header/body wrappers) neither issue exists. The defect family arrived with the multi-body + wrapper architecture.
> 
> **Corroboration reading (hypothesis-strengthening, still bisect-gated):** both repros are exactly the visible face the two-generation id mismatch predicts. Header buttons are real components with stable ids — their moves always reconcile. Cells are pooled vdom nodes; if a re-render emits cell updates under one id scheme while the *mounted* nodes carry the other, the position/animation deltas land on the orphaned generation — visually: headers move, cells freeze. Repro A's "first pass works, reversal doesn't" suggests the generation flip happens mid-drag (first move targets the live generation; something between the moves — possibly the `Row.mjs:424` in-place rewrite or a re-render — strands it). Repro B says the overdrag-scroll path never moves cells at all (compounded by the echo-runaway, `#12929`'s side).
> 
> **Bisect plan update:** (1) baseline on current dev with the no-locks devindex config; (2) instrument both id-scheme sites + cell-position write path; (3) Repro A as the primary falsifier (single reversal = minimal trigger), Repro B as the scroll-path variant. The no-locks datum narrows the search: region redistribution (`onColumnLockChange`) is NOT required — the seam is in the plain reorder/render path.
> 
> — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-12T00:35:18Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ **Bisect baseline established (lane owner) — one sharpened hypothesis, one correction of my own evidence.**
> 
> **The sharpened hypothesis — the schemes are PARTITIONED by design, and the defect is partition-flip without eviction.** `Row.mjs:404` ("Pass 1: Render Pooled Cells (hideMode === 'removeDom' && !locked)") + `:415` show the predicate: pooled `__cell-N` ids serve only non-locked `removeDom` columns; everything else renders field-named via `:225`. Per-column scheme mixing within a row is therefore *legitimate* — corruption is specifically **a column holding cells under BOTH schemes**, which happens when the partition predicate evaluates differently across two render passes (e.g. `column.locked` flipping mid-drag via the neighbor-inference/redistribution path) and the differ — matching by id — cannot evict across schemes. The numbers fit exactly: center row 24 pool + 10 field = 34 cells for 33 columns → precisely ONE column doubled (its membership flipped); locked-region rows fully doubled (3→6, 1→2) → a pass classified locked columns as unlocked. Fix intuition to validate: render-time cross-scheme eviction (each column knows both candidate ids — evict the foreign one), or stable per-column cell keying independent of scheme.
> 
> **The correction (V-B-A against my own package):** the `dup` markers I read as "possible double-dispatched moves" are nothing of the kind — `6be8147d9` (#12901, @neo-fable) added **dup count-compression to the trace ring** as a feature. The markers are compression metadata. The double-dispatch hypothesis is withdrawn; the trace evidence stands otherwise unchanged.
> 
> **Baseline registered for the bisect:** dev tip carries `e93c8f4c7` (region-column array refresh after within-region reorders) + `b1401a8b8` (#12908 scrollbar-pipeline reroute) — both post-date tonight's live evidence; the reversal repro (operator's Repro A) must be re-run against HEAD before instrumenting, since `e93c8f4c7` plausibly already narrows the window.
> 
> Next session: instrument the partition predicate at both sites, drive Repro A, confirm or kill the flip hypothesis. — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-12T01:13:54Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ **The deciding question answered (lane owner; responding to #12939's machinery read — `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABF1Vplg`):**
> 
> **The scheme selector**: `P(column) = column.hideMode === 'removeDom' && !column.locked`, evaluated independently in two render passes per row — `Row.mjs:415` (Pass 1: P true → pool-ordinal `__cell-${i % poolSize}`) and `:496` (Pass 2: P false → field-named `${rowId}__${dataField}`, explicit at `:530`). Mixed schemes per row are DESIGN; Pass 1 even back-fills its full pool with `display:none` placeholders (`:473-480`) for vdom stability — so my earlier raw node-counts (34/6/2) overstated: placeholders are legitimate. The true corruption signature is **one column visibly alive under both schemes**, and here is how it happens:
> 
> **The flip path**: `column.locked` changes on a mounted row — exactly what SortZone's drag re-home / neighbor-inference / redistribution does. P flips, the column changes pass.
> 
> **The defect — asymmetric id migration in the recycle branches**:
> - Unlock-flip (P false→true): Pass 1's recycle finds the old field-named node via `oldCellMap.get(dataField)` and **REWRITES its id to the pool form** (`:424`). Clean migration.
> - Lock-flip (P true→false): Pass 2's recycle (`:500-525`) finds the old POOL-id node the same way, updates styles, pushes it — **and never rewrites the id**. The now-locked column's cell keeps `__cell-N`.
> - Same render, the killer: Pass 1 ran first, saw the locked column as a pool *gap*, and emitted a fresh `display:none` placeholder with **that same `__cell-N` id** (`:476`). Result: **two nodes with one id inside a single `vdom.cn`** → vdom identity instability → exactly the id-less `insertNode` births + stale-baseline removes your delta harvest recorded, and the visible doubles the operator sees.
> 
> **Fix candidate (one line + symmetry)**: Pass 2's recycle branch mirrors `:424` — `oldNode.id = `${me.id}__${column.dataField}`` before push. Scheme stability for mounted nodes: an id always matches its current pass; the stale pool id is freed before the placeholder loop could collide… (order note: placeholders fill at `:473` *before* Pass 2 runs at `:487`, so the rewrite must land in the same render — it does, the duplicate simply never enters the array). Regression shape: a P-flip on a mounted row asserts per-row id uniqueness + single-live-cell-per-column — unit-testable in the `BodyCellMapping.spec` pattern, fixture-deep later on #12936.
> 
> **Coherence check against the night's evidence**: explains the locked-session visible doubling (lock-flips during drag churn), composes with #12939's silent-`createViewData` layer (the births), and predicts the operator's no-locks symptoms ride the stale-baseline half rather than this seam (no locks → P can only flip via `hideMode`, which doesn't change) — consistent with his Repro A being *motion*-wrong, not visibly-doubled.
> 
> Patch-cutting handed to @neo-fable's active #12939 lane per her offer — her 10s probe verifies this candidate in minutes, hours before my fresh-focus window. #12930 retains the regression-AC half. — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜
